remember: Students will be able to recall the plot and setting of the story 'Evans Tries an O-Level' by Jack Rosenthal.
Students will be able to identify the main characters, their roles, and relationships in the story.
Students will be able to remember key events and turning points in the narrative.
understand: Students will be able to understand the social context in which the story is set, highlighting the themes of social class and identity.
Students will be able to analyze the use of language and tone in the story to convey the author's message.
Students will be able to comprehend the character of Evans and his struggles with his identity and social status.
apply: Students will be able to apply their understanding of the story to analyze the themes and messages conveyed by the author.
Students will be able to use the story to discuss the social issues of the time period and their relevance to the present day.
Students will be able to apply the literary devices used in the story to interpret the author's intentions and meaning.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the character of Evans and his relationships with other characters to understand his development and growth throughout the story.
Students will be able to analyze the use of dialogue and conversation in the story to reveal character traits and relationships.
Students will be able to analyze the themes and messages of the story to evaluate the author's views on social class and identity.

ncert_objectives: To understand the plot and setting of the story 'Evans Tries an O-Level' and its relevance to the themes of social class and identity.
To analyze the character of Evans and his struggles with his identity and social status.
To evaluate the use of language and tone in the story to convey the author's message.
board_exam_objectives: To recall and describe the plot, setting, and main characters of the story 'Evans Tries an O-Level'.
To understand and analyze the themes and messages conveyed by the author, including social class and identity.
To evaluate the literary devices used in the story and their significance in conveying the author's intentions and meaning.
